acceptance_targets: [chapter_06_prose_01] Pass if the revised chapter no longer uses any one negation-emphasis frame more than twice across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:71 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:387, and at least half of the cited beats are rewritten as direct positive description rather than definition-by-negation. Fail if repeated 'It was not...' / 'He did not...' / 'There was...' structures still dominate the same emphasis points.

[chapter_06_prose_02] Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:309, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:371 is grammatically complete and still urgent when read aloud. Fail if subjectless fragment beats such as 'No time' or single-noun declaratives remain in the narrative voice.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter no longer uses any one negation-emphasis frame more than twice across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:71 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:387, and at least half of the cited beats are rewritten as direct positive description rather than definition-by-negation. Fail if repeated 'It was not...' / 'He did not...' / 'There was...' structures still dominate the same emphasis points.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on negation-led emphasis: 'It was not...', 'He did not...', 'There was...', and 'not X ... but Y.' Used once or twice, that syntax sharpens a beat; used this often, it becomes a detectable prose tic and drains force from moments that should feel freshly observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast a substantial share of the flagged sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:71, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:127, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:245, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:269, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:377, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:387 into affirmative sensory or action-led description. Keep negation only where refusal or self-correction is itself the point of the sentence.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:309, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:371 is grammatically complete and still urgent when read aloud. Fail if subjectless fragment beats such as 'No time' or single-noun declaratives remain in the narrative voice.
findings.problem: The chapter slips into clipped narration fragments ('No time. No clean wording. No safe radius.' / 'Pots. Water.' / 'One note.') even though the constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration. In a chapter whose baseline prose is otherwise controlled and grammatical, these fragments read as mannered compression rather than earned urgency.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the narration at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:309, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:371 into full grammatical sentences or clearly framed interior thought. Preserve speed and pressure through sentence rhythm, not by dropping subjects or function words.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the premise's sharpest contradiction: school-year comedy, adolescent awkwardness, punitive cruelty, and cosmic dread may coexist in the same novel without canceling each other. When a scene offers a cleaner version and a truer version, choose the truer one unless the character is actively refusing perception on the page.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If pain, cruelty, fear, or moral ugliness would leave a bodily or environmental trace, render that trace instead of summarizing it into tasteful haze.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High. Contractions are the spoken norm for most characters; fuller forms are reserved for Snape, ritual language, or deliberate emphasis.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate scene density, dialogue load, and movement mode aggressively: confrontation against quiet meals, subterranean revelation against corridor farce, ritual compression against spring drift, so no consecutive chapters feel built from the same emotional or structural template.
prose_style_profile.diction: British boarding-school realism under wartime pressure, with speculative vocabulary introduced only when Owen's attention earns it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third limited through Owen, with only brief sensory widenings that he could plausibly notice in the moment.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate analytic accumulation with blunt corporeal sentences. Quiet chapters should breathe; punishment and ritual chapters should tighten without clipping grammar.
